back_translation: When Sanballat, Tobiah, the Arabs, the Ammonites, and the Ashdodites heard that the repair of the walls of Jerusalem was progressing and that the gaps were being closed up, they became very angry.
All of them conspired together to come and make war against Jerusalem and to cause confusion.
So we prayed to our God and stationed a guard against them both day and night.
Then the people of Judah said, "The strength of the carriers has weakened, and there is much rubble; we cannot continue building the wall."
And our enemies said, "Before they know or see anything, we will go into their midst, kill them, and put an end to the work."
When the Jews who lived near the enemies came to us, they said to us ten times from all the places where they lived, "They will come and attack us."
So I stationed people at the lowest sections behind the wall, in the exposed places. I arranged them by family clans with their swords, spears, and bows.

When I had inspected the situation, I rose up and said to the nobles, the officials, and the rest of the people, "Do not be afraid of them. Remember the Sovereign-Master, the great and awesome, and fight for your brothers, sons, daughters, wives, and homes."
When our enemies heard that we knew their plan, and that God had made their counsel of no effect, we all returned to our work on the wall.
From that day on, half of my servants did the work, and the other half held spears, shields, bows, and armor. The commanders stood behind all the household of Judah.
Those who built the wall and those who carried materials kept working — one hand doing the work, the other hand holding a weapon.
Each of the builders had his sword strapped to his waist as he built. And the trumpeter stayed beside me.
I said to the nobles, the officials, and the rest of the people, "The work is great and extensive, and we are separated along the wall, far from one another.
Wherever you hear the sound of the trumpet, gather to us there. Our God will fight for us."
So we continued the work, and half of them held spears from the rising of dawn until the stars came out.
At that time I also said to the people, "Let every man and his servant lodge for the night inside Jerusalem, so they may be guards for us at night and workers by day."
Neither I, nor my brothers, nor my servants, nor the guards who were with me — none of us took off our clothes; even when going for water, each one carried his weapon.
